title: eulophia litoralis
intro: Eulophia litoralis, also known as the coastal eulophia or the sea maxillaria, is a type of terrestrial orchid that belongs to the family Orchidaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for treating respiratory issues such as coughs and colds, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is used to relieve various health conditions, including bronchitis, asthma, and skin problems, thanks to its antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. Eulophia litoralis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or extracts, which are often used to promote respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of various ailments.
